name: Encinas Reservoir Loop
name_original: Embalse de Encinas — circular desde Encinas de Esgueva
description: Hike a moderate 5.5-mile (8.8 km) circular route around the tranquil Embalse de Encinas, gaining 310 feet (95 metres) in about 2 hours 20 mi
region_intro: Hiking around Encinas De Esgueva offers varied terrain centered around the Esgueva River valley. The landscape features accessible paths, notably around the Encinas de Esgueva Reservoir. Trails often include gentle elevation changes and views of the surrounding countryside. The region provides a network of routes suitable for different fitness levels. Best hiking trails around Encinas De Esgueva The most popular hiking route is The Springs Trail, an 8.7 miles (14.0 km) trail that takes 3 hours 45 minutes to complete. This moderate route explores springs and offers valley views. Another top favourite among local hikers is Encinas Reservoir Loop, a moderate 5.5 miles (8.8 km) path. This circular route provides views of the tranquil reservoir and surrounding natural features. Local hikers also love the Embalse de Encinas de Esgueva, a 4.3 miles (6.9 km) trail leading through the reservoir area, often completed in about 1 hour 49 minutes. Hiking around Encinas De Esgueva is defined by reservoir paths, valley views, and accessible trails, with options for various ability levels. The routes in Encinas De Esgueva are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.8 stars from more than 4 reviews. More than 30 hikers have used komoot to explore Encinas De Esgueva's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Encinas De Esgueva?
A: There are over 10 hiking routes around Encinas De Esgueva. These include 4 easy trails and 6 moderate options, ensuring there's something for various fitness levels.

Q: Are there any easy or beginner-friendly hiking routes in the area?
A: Yes, Encinas De Esgueva offers several easy trails suitable for beginners. A good option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1077647364/embalse-de-encinas-de-esgueva" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Embalse de Encinas de Esgueva&lt;/a&gt; route, which is 4.3 miles (6.9 km) long and typically takes less than two hours to complete with minimal elevation gain.

Q: Can I find circular hiking routes around Encinas De Esgueva?
A: Yes, circular routes are a prominent feature of hiking in this region. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1040935289/encinas-reservoir-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Encinas Reservoir Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a popular moderate 5.5-mile (8.8 km) path that offers scenic views of the tranquil reservoir. Another option is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1037354194/part-of-the-mulberry-and-valleys-trail-circular-from-villovela-de-esgueva" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Part of the Mulberry and Valleys Trail - circular from Villovela de Esgueva&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What are the main natural features or landscapes I can expect to see on a hike?
A: Hiking around Encinas De Esgueva is characterized by the Esgueva River valley, the serene Encinas de Esgueva Reservoir, and the scenic Valle del Cuco. Many trails offer views of these natural features, including springs and varied countryside.

Q: Are there any family-friendly hiking options?
A: The area provides accessible paths suitable for various skill levels, including families. Trails around the Encinas de Esgueva Reservoir, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1077647364/embalse-de-encinas-de-esgueva" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Embalse de Encinas de Esgueva&lt;/a&gt;, are generally easy and offer gentle elevation changes, making them suitable for outings with children.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Encinas De Esgueva?
A: The routes in Encinas De Esgueva are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.8 stars from over 4 reviews. Hikers often praise the varied terrain, the tranquil reservoir views, and the accessible nature of the paths.

Q: Are there any historical sites or landmarks to explore near the hiking trails?
A: Yes, the region has several points of interest. You can find castles such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4384200" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle of the Zúñiga Family (Encinas de Esgueva)&lt;/a&gt; located near the reservoir. Other nearby attractions include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5507727" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Curiel de Duero Castle&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5617069" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Wooden Bridge over the Duero River&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the typical elevation gain on hikes in this region?
A: Elevation gains vary across the trails. Many routes, especially around the reservoir, feature gentle elevation changes, often under 100 meters. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1040935289/encinas-reservoir-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Encinas Reservoir Loop&lt;/a&gt; has an elevation gain of about 95 meters. More challenging routes may involve greater ascents.

Q: Are there any trails that feature springs or unique water features?
A: Yes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1077610408/the-springs-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Springs Trail&lt;/a&gt; is an 8.7-mile (14.0 km) circular route specifically designed to explore springs and enjoy valley views from Corrales de Duero.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Encinas De Esgueva?
A: The region offers pleasant hiking conditions for much of the year. Spring and autumn generally provide mild temperatures and vibrant scenery, making them ideal. Summer can be warm, so early morning or late afternoon hikes are recommended. Winter hikes are also possible, though some paths might be affected by weather conditions.

Q: Are there any longer, more challenging hikes for experienced hikers?
A: While many trails are moderate, some routes offer longer distances and slightly more varied terrain. For instance, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1077610408/the-springs-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Springs Trail&lt;/a&gt; is 8.7 miles (14.0 km) with an elevation gain of about 170 meters, providing a good challenge for those seeking a longer outing.

Q: Is parking available at the trailheads?
A: While specific parking details for every trailhead are not provided, many popular hiking areas, especially around the Encinas de Esgueva Reservoir, typically have designated parking facilities to accommodate visitors.
tips: The Encinas de Esgueva dam is an irrigation engineering work built in the course of the Arroyo de la Dehesa, also called Eras, which flows into the Esgueva river.

A town that is said to have been populated in large quantities with holm oaks in other times, and that I now live on the path of Esgueva. This valley begins its journey here through the province of Valladolid along 55 kilometers to the capital.

faqs.question: What is the typical duration for hiking the Embalse de Encinas circular route?
faqs.answer: This circular route around the Encinas Reservoir typically takes around 2 hours and 21 minutes to complete, depending on your pace and how often you stop to enjoy the scenery.

faqs.question: Is the Embalse de Encinas trail suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: Yes, while rated as moderate, the trail features easily accessible paths and well-maintained dirt tracks. Parts of the route are completely flat, making it suitable for various skill levels, including those new to hiking, provided they have good physical fitness.

faqs.question: Where can I park when visiting the Embalse de Encinas trail?
faqs.answer: Parking is generally available near the reservoir or in the village of Encinas de Esgueva. The trail is well-signposted and accessible from the village, which is a good starting point.

faqs.question: Is the Embalse de Encinas — circular desde Encinas de Esgueva trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, this trail is generally dog-friendly. However, it's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, especially around wildlife and other hikers, and to clean up after them.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this route?
faqs.answer: The trail can be enjoyed year-round. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery. Winter is excellent for birdwatching, as various species like cormorants and coots gather at the reservoir. During summer, you can also enjoy swimming and other water activities at the reservoir.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee to hike the Embalse de Encinas trail?
faqs.answer: No, you do not need a permit, and there are no entrance fees to access the Embalse de Encinas trail. It is freely accessible to the public.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on the Embalse de Encinas circular route?
faqs.answer: The terrain consists mainly of easily accessible paths and well-maintained dirt tracks. Some sections are completely flat, offering a comfortable walking surface around the reservoir.

faqs.question: What wildlife and natural features can I see along the way?
faqs.answer: You can observe various bird species, especially during winter, including ducks, hoopoes, bee-eaters, cormorants, coots, and laughing gulls. The route also features lush poplar groves and a growing presence of holm oaks (encinas). The main natural feature is the tranquil, greenish-blue expanse of the Encinas Reservoir itself.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities or services available near the trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, during summer, a bar and camping facilities are available near the reservoir. The nearby village of Encinas de Esgueva also offers services, including historical sites like the 15th-century Aguilar Castle and a church.

faqs.question: Does the Embalse de Encinas trail connect with other hiking paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, this trail is part of a wider network, including the "Mulberry and Valleys Trail." A small section (1%) of the route also follows the Camino Valderromán.

faqs.question: Are there shaded areas or places to rest along the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, you'll find a shaded area with benches and tables along the route, perfect for a rest or a picnic. There's also a small beach area by the reservoir that can be used for relaxation during summer.
